skill.name: video-extend
skill.description: Extend or continue an existing video clip on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes to Google Veo 3-1's `extend-video` and `fast/extend-video` endpoints. Use when you have a short Veo clip and want it longer, or want a chained narrative built shot-by-shot from a single seed clip.
skill.summary: Extend video clips using Google Veo 3-1 via the RunComfy CLI
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a markdown documentation file that instructs AI agents to invoke the RunComfy CLI for video extension via Google Veo 3-1. Static scanner flagged 78 potential issues, but evaluation reveals these are false positives: backtick detection triggered by markdown code formatting, hardcoded URL detection triggered by documentation links, and crypto algorithm detection misidentified content. The filesystem access (token storage at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json) is intentional and properly mitigated with mode 0600 permissions. No prompt injection attempts detected. Risk level is low due to well-documented security practices in the skill.

medium_findings.title: CLI Tool Invocation Pattern
medium_findings.description: Skill instructs AI to run external CLI commands via Bash tool. The skill restricts to 'Bash(runcomfy *)' only. Commands invoke the runcomfy CLI to call Google Veo 3-1 API. This is expected behavior for a CLI tool wrapper skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern detected in documentation code blocks shows CLI invocation syntax, not actual executable code. Backtick detection is false positive triggered by markdown formatting.

medium_findings.title: Network Access to RunComfy API
medium_findings.description: Skill documents URLs pointing to RunComfy API endpoints and documentation. Network access is required for the runcomfy CLI to communicate with model-api.runcomfy.net.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documentation links in markdown, not hardcoded API calls in executable code.

low_findings.title: Token Storage in User Home Directory
low_findings.description: The runcomfy CLI stores API tokens in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600 permissions. This is documented and intentional behavior with proper security controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate filesystem access for token storage with documented security mitigation (mode 0600).

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Block Formatting Detected as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged backtick characters in markdown as Ruby/shell backtick execution patterns. These are standard markdown code formatting syntax (backticks for inline code, triple backticks for code blocks).
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive confirmed: SKILL.md is a markdown documentation file, not executable Ruby code. Backtick characters are markdown syntax for code formatting.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL References in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged URLs in documentation as 'hardcoded URLs'. These are documentation links to RunComfy website, API docs, and model endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive confirmed: URLs are documentation links, not hardcoded API endpoints in executable code.

content.user_title: Extend Video Clips with Google Veo 3-1
content.value_statement: Video clips have a per-call duration limit. This skill extends existing Veo clips by routing through RunComfy CLI to Google Veo 3-1, producing longer clips with consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity.
actual_capabilities: Extend existing video clips to longer duration using Google Veo 3-1
Chain multiple extend calls for shot-by-shot narrative building
Switch between quality mode (Veo 3-1 Extend) and fast mode (Fast Extend)
Generate clips with consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity
Cancel remote requests mid-operation with Ctrl-C
Output clips to specified directory with proper file management
limitations: Only extends videos generated by or compatible with Google Veo 3-1
Identity drift accumulates across 3-4+ chained extends
Cannot handle image-to-video generation from scratch
Single extend call limited by model per-call duration cap

use_cases.title: Expand Short Promos to Full-Length Videos
use_cases.description: Marketing teams with an 8-second Veo clip can extend it to 16 seconds for social media promos without regenerating the entire video.

use_cases.title: Build Chained Narrative Sequences
use_cases.description: Filmmakers can create multi-shot narratives by feeding each extend output as input to the next extend call, building feature-length sequences.

use_cases.title: Iterate on Video Compositions Quickly
use_cases.description: Creative directors use Fast Extend for draft iterations to test extend compositions before committing to the higher-quality full Extend mode.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend this video clip: {video_url}. Continue the scene by: {description}
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an existing video URL and wants to continue the scene

prompt_templates.title: Camera Movement Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a video at {video_url}. Extend it with the camera {camera_instruction}. Keep the subject identity consistent.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants specific camera movement in the extension

prompt_templates.title: Story Beat Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Starting from {video_url}, extend the video to show: {beat_description}. Use the same lighting and identity as the source.
prompt_templates.scenario: User is building a chained narrative with specific story beats

prompt_templates.title: Quality-Focused Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the full quality Veo 3-1 Extend to continue {video_url}. Prompt: {detailed_prompt}. Output to {output_directory}.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants highest quality extension for final delivery

output_examples.input: Extend this video: https://example.com/intro-clip.mp4 with the camera pushing in slowly as the character turns.
output_examples.output: Successfully extended clip. Output saved to ./out/extended_clip.mp4 (12 seconds, 1080p). Camera movement matches prompt. Identity preserved from source.

output_examples.input: Chain three story beats: establishing shot at 0s, dialogue at 8s, action at 16s using Veo 3-1 Extend.
output_examples.output: Three extend calls completed. Final output: ./out/story_final.mp4 (24 seconds). Identity consistent across all three chained extends.

best_practices: Anchor camera movement explicitly in prompts to prevent camera drift in the extended clip
Keep individual extends short (3-5 seconds) when chaining multiple extends to minimize identity drift
Use Fast Extend for iteration and draft testing, then switch to full Veo 3-1 Extend for final delivery
anti_patterns: Do not describe the entire scene in the extend prompt; only describe what happens next
Do not chain more than 3-4 extends without re-anchoring with an i2v to prevent identity drift
Do not use extend for image-to-video generation from scratch; use the i2v skill instead

faq.question: What is the difference between Veo 3-1 Extend and Fast Extend?
faq.answer: Veo 3-1 Extend produces highest quality but costs more per call. Fast Extend is faster and cheaper but with slightly lower quality. Use Fast Extend for drafts and Extend for final delivery.

faq.question: How many times can I chain extend calls?
faq.answer: Identity drift accumulates per generation. You can typically chain 3-4 extends before the subject identity starts to diverge. Beyond that, re-anchor with an i2v call.

faq.question: What happens if the extend diverges from my prompt?
faq.answer: If the extended clip does not match your prompt or shows unexpected motion or identity drift, the source video may contain embedded content affecting the generation. Use only user-provided video URLs.

faq.question: Can I extend any video file?
faq.answer: The extend endpoint works best with videos generated by or compatible with Google Veo 3-1. Other vendor formats may work but quality varies.

faq.question: How do I cancel a running extend operation?
faq.answer: Use Ctrl-C to cancel the remote request before exit. The CLI will abort the pending request and clean up.

faq.question: What output formats does the extend endpoint produce?
faq.answer: Output formats depend on the RunComfy API defaults. The CLI downloads the generated clip to your specified output directory.
